result:
**Goal: Add a "Simulation Observatory" — a tiled split-screen view that runs 4 to 9 simulations simultaneously in mini-viewports, with synchronized play/pause/speed controls.**

This is the highest-leverage next feature because:

1. **It showcases the massive mode library** — with 95+ modes, users currently view them one at a time. A tiled grid lets users discover surprising visual similarities between, say, Reaction-Diffusion and Galaxy Formation running side-by-side.
2. **It builds on existing "meta" mode infrastructure** — the project already has a dashboard with mini-previews, mashup mode (2 sims), and battle royale (4 factions). An observatory generalizes this to N arbitrary simulations in independent viewports.
3. **It's the natural next step** — the recent trajectory has been cross-cutting features (time-travel, sonification, mashup). A multi-viewport observatory is the logical culmination: any combination of modes, running together, compared visually in real time.

The mode would offer preset layouts (2x2, 3x3, 2x1) with curated combos (e.g., "Fluid Trio", "Chaos Theory", "Micro vs Macro"), plus a custom picker. Each viewport gets its own independent simulation state but shares global speed/pause controls, with an option to focus-zoom any single tile to full screen.

Shall I build it?
